---
title: "FW 536 — Day 3 afternoon lab (v2 · terrestrial wildlife) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 3 afternoon lab — Model selection problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — terrestrial wildlife theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — aquatic & fisheries](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(nlme)
library(AICcmodavg)
library(MuMIn)
```

---

# Problem 6 — Sage-grouse lek attendance: nested fixed-effect comparison

> You have 8 years of lek-attendance counts on 30 leks. Candidate fixed effects: year (continuous), spring_precip, and their interaction. Lek is a random intercept.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 7 — Bighorn sheep lamb survival: a dredge gone wrong

> A graduate student fits a logistic regression of lamb survival on 12 candidate predictors (elevation, slope, aspect, vegetation indices, distance-to-water, etc.) on 90 lambs. They run dredge() and find a top model containing 5 predictors with $\Delta\text{AICc} = 0$. They repo...

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 8 — Coyote pup survival: when BIC misses the weak effect

> You want to know whether 3 predictors affect coyote pup survival: litter_size (strong known effect), maternal_age (modest effect), and winter_severity (weak effect). $n = 60$ pups. Simulate:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 9 — Pronghorn fawn:doe ratios — predicting next year's index

> You have 15 years of pronghorn fawn:doe ratios, with annual covariates: june_precip, winter_snow, coyote_index. You want to predict next year's ratio.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 10 — Desert tortoise survival: when not to model-average

> You fit 6 candidate logistic models of tortoise survival. Three have nearly identical AICc ($\Delta\text{AICc} < 1$); the rest are far behind. A reviewer asks for "model-averaged predictions" using Akaike weights. The three competitive models contain different combinations of ...

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every afternoon problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
